# math

```go
import "math"
```

Package math provides basic constants and mathematical functions.

This package does not guarantee bit\-identical results across architectures.

## Constants

<a name="E"></a>Mathematical constants.

```go
const (
    E   = 2.71828182845904523536028747135266249775724709369995957496696763 // https://oeis.org/A001113
    Pi  = 3.14159265358979323846264338327950288419716939937510582097494459 // https://oeis.org/A000796
    Phi = 1.61803398874989484820458683436563811772030917980576286213544862 // https://oeis.org/A001622

    Sqrt2   = 1.41421356237309504880168872420969807856967187537694807317667974 // https://oeis.org/A002193
    SqrtE   = 1.64872127070012814684865078781416357165377610071014801157507931 // https://oeis.org/A019774
    SqrtPi  = 1.77245385090551602729816748334114518279754945612238712821380779 // https://oeis.org/A002161
    SqrtPhi = 1.27201964951406896425242246173749149171560804184009624861664038 // https://oeis.org/A139339

    Ln2    = 0.693147180559945309417232121458176568075500134360255254120680009 // https://oeis.org/A002162
    Log2E  = 1 / Ln2
    Ln10   = 2.30258509299404568401799145468436420760110148862877297603332790 // https://oeis.org/A002392
    Log10E = 1 / Ln10
)
```

<a name="MaxFloat32"></a>Floating\-point limit values. Max is the largest finite value representable by the type. SmallestNonzero is the smallest positive, non\-zero value representable by the type.

```go
const (
    MaxFloat32             = 0x1p127 * (1 + (1 - 0x1p-23)) // 3.40282346638528859811704183484516925440e+38
    SmallestNonzeroFloat32 = 0x1p-126 * 0x1p-23            // 1.401298464324817070923729583289916131280e-45

    MaxFloat64             = 0x1p1023 * (1 + (1 - 0x1p-52)) // 1.79769313486231570814527423731704356798070e+308
    SmallestNonzeroFloat64 = 0x1p-1022 * 0x1p-52            // 4.9406564584124654417656879286822137236505980e-324
)
```

<a name="MaxInt"></a>Integer limit values.

```go
const (
    MaxInt    = 1<<(intSize-1) - 1  // MaxInt32 or MaxInt64 depending on intSize.
    MinInt    = -1 << (intSize - 1) // MinInt32 or MinInt64 depending on intSize.
    MaxInt8   = 1<<7 - 1            // 127
    MinInt8   = -1 << 7             // -128
    MaxInt16  = 1<<15 - 1           // 32767
    MinInt16  = -1 << 15            // -32768
    MaxInt32  = 1<<31 - 1           // 2147483647
    MinInt32  = -1 << 31            // -2147483648
    MaxInt64  = 1<<63 - 1           // 9223372036854775807
    MinInt64  = -1 << 63            // -9223372036854775808
    MaxUint   = 1<<intSize - 1      // MaxUint32 or MaxUint64 depending on intSize.
    MaxUint8  = 1<<8 - 1            // 255
    MaxUint16 = 1<<16 - 1           // 65535
    MaxUint32 = 1<<32 - 1           // 4294967295
    MaxUint64 = 1<<64 - 1           // 18446744073709551615
)
```

<a name="Abs"></a>

## func [Abs](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/abs.go#L13>)

```go
func Abs(x float64) float64
```

Abs returns the absolute value of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Abs(±Inf) = +Inf
Abs(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	x := math.Abs(-2)
	fmt.Printf("%.1f\n", x)

	y := math.Abs(2)
	fmt.Printf("%.1f\n", y)
}
```

#### Output

```go
2.0
2.0
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Acos"></a>

## func [Acos](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/asin.go#L58>)

```go
func Acos(x float64) float64
```

Acos returns the arccosine, in radians, of x.

Special case is:

```go
Acos(x) = NaN if x < -1 or x > 1
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f", math.Acos(1))
}
```

#### Output

```go
0.00
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Acosh"></a>

## func [Acosh](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/acosh.go#L43>)

```go
func Acosh(x float64) float64
```

Acosh returns the inverse hyperbolic cosine of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Acosh(+Inf) = +Inf
Acosh(x) = NaN if x < 1
Acosh(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f", math.Acosh(1))
}
```

#### Output

```go
0.00
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Asin"></a>

## func [Asin](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/asin.go#L20>)

```go
func Asin(x float64) float64
```

Asin returns the arcsine, in radians, of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Asin(±0) = ±0
Asin(x) = NaN if x < -1 or x > 1
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f", math.Asin(0))
}
```

#### Output

```go
0.00
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Asinh"></a>

## func [Asinh](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/asinh.go#L40>)

```go
func Asinh(x float64) float64
```

Asinh returns the inverse hyperbolic sine of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Asinh(±0) = ±0
Asinh(±Inf) = ±Inf
Asinh(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f", math.Asinh(0))
}
```

#### Output

```go
0.00
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Atan"></a>

## func [Atan](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/atan.go#L96>)

```go
func Atan(x float64) float64
```

Atan returns the arctangent, in radians, of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Atan(±0) = ±0
Atan(±Inf) = ±Pi/2
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f", math.Atan(0))
}
```

#### Output

```go
0.00
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Atan2"></a>

## func [Atan2](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/atan2.go#L30>)

```go
func Atan2(y, x float64) float64
```

Atan2 returns the arc tangent of y/x, using the signs of the two to determine the quadrant of the return value.

Special cases are \(in order\):

```go
Atan2(y, NaN) = NaN
Atan2(NaN, x) = NaN
Atan2(+0, x>=0) = +0
Atan2(-0, x>=0) = -0
Atan2(+0, x<=-0) = +Pi
Atan2(-0, x<=-0) = -Pi
Atan2(y>0, 0) = +Pi/2
Atan2(y<0, 0) = -Pi/2
Atan2(+Inf, +Inf) = +Pi/4
Atan2(-Inf, +Inf) = -Pi/4
Atan2(+Inf, -Inf) = 3Pi/4
Atan2(-Inf, -Inf) = -3Pi/4
Atan2(y, +Inf) = 0
Atan2(y>0, -Inf) = +Pi
Atan2(y<0, -Inf) = -Pi
Atan2(+Inf, x) = +Pi/2
Atan2(-Inf, x) = -Pi/2
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f", math.Atan2(0, 0))
}
```

#### Output

```go
0.00
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Atanh"></a>

## func [Atanh](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/atanh.go#L48>)

```go
func Atanh(x float64) float64
```

Atanh returns the inverse hyperbolic tangent of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Atanh(1) = +Inf
Atanh(±0) = ±0
Atanh(-1) = -Inf
Atanh(x) = NaN if x < -1 or x > 1
Atanh(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f", math.Atanh(0))
}
```

#### Output

```go
0.00
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Cbrt"></a>

## func [Cbrt](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/cbrt.go#L26>)

```go
func Cbrt(x float64) float64
```

Cbrt returns the cube root of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Cbrt(±0) = ±0
Cbrt(±Inf) = ±Inf
Cbrt(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f\n", math.Cbrt(8))
	fmt.Printf("%.2f\n", math.Cbrt(27))
}
```

#### Output

```go
2.00
3.00
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Ceil"></a>

## func [Ceil](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/floor.go#L43>)

```go
func Ceil(x float64) float64
```

Ceil returns the least integer value greater than or equal to x.

Special cases are:

```go
Ceil(±0) = ±0
Ceil(±Inf) = ±Inf
Ceil(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	c := math.Ceil(1.49)
	fmt.Printf("%.1f", c)
}
```

#### Output

```go
2.0
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Copysign"></a>

## func [Copysign](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/copysign.go#L9>)

```go
func Copysign(f, sign float64) float64
```

Copysign returns a value with the magnitude of f and the sign of sign.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f", math.Copysign(3.2, -1))
}
```

#### Output

```go
-3.20
```

</p>

<a name="Cos"></a>

## func [Cos](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/sin.go#L118>)

```go
func Cos(x float64) float64
```

Cos returns the cosine of the radian argument x.

Special cases are:

```go
Cos(±Inf) = NaN
Cos(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f", math.Cos(math.Pi/2))
}
```

#### Output

```go

```

</p>

<a name="Cosh"></a>

## func [Cosh](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/sinh.go#L79>)

```go
func Cosh(x float64) float64
```

Cosh returns the hyperbolic cosine of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Cosh(±0) = 1
Cosh(±Inf) = +Inf
Cosh(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f", math.Cosh(0))
}
```

#### Output

```go
1.00
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Dim"></a>

## func [Dim](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/dim.go#L14>)

```go
func Dim(x, y float64) float64
```

Dim returns the maximum of x\-y or 0.

Special cases are:

```go
Dim(+Inf, +Inf) = NaN
Dim(-Inf, -Inf) = NaN
Dim(x, NaN) = Dim(NaN, x) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f\n", math.Dim(4, -2))
	fmt.Printf("%.2f\n", math.Dim(-4, 2))
}
```

#### Output

```go
6.00
0.00
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Erf"></a>

## func [Erf](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/erf.go#L189>)

```go
func Erf(x float64) float64
```

Erf returns the error function of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Erf(+Inf) = 1
Erf(-Inf) = -1
Erf(NaN) = NaN
```

<a name="Erfc"></a>

## func [Erfc](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/erf.go#L274>)

```go
func Erfc(x float64) float64
```

Erfc returns the complementary error function of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Erfc(+Inf) = 0
Erfc(-Inf) = 2
Erfc(NaN) = NaN
```

<a name="Erfcinv"></a>

## func [Erfcinv](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/erfinv.go#L127>)

```go
func Erfcinv(x float64) float64
```

Erfcinv returns the inverse of [Erfc](<#Erfc>)\(x\).

Special cases are:

```go
Erfcinv(0) = +Inf
Erfcinv(2) = -Inf
Erfcinv(x) = NaN if x < 0 or x > 2
Erfcinv(NaN) = NaN
```

<a name="Erfinv"></a>

## func [Erfinv](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/erfinv.go#L77>)

```go
func Erfinv(x float64) float64
```

Erfinv returns the inverse error function of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Erfinv(1) = +Inf
Erfinv(-1) = -Inf
Erfinv(x) = NaN if x < -1 or x > 1
Erfinv(NaN) = NaN
```

<a name="Exp"></a>

## func [Exp](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/exp.go#L16>)

```go
func Exp(x float64) float64
```

Exp returns e\*\*x, the base\-e exponential of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Exp(+Inf) = +Inf
Exp(NaN) = NaN
```

Very large values overflow to 0 or \+Inf. Very small values underflow to 1.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f\n", math.Exp(1))
	fmt.Printf("%.2f\n", math.Exp(2))
	fmt.Printf("%.2f\n", math.Exp(-1))
}
```

#### Output

```go
2.72
7.39
0.37
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Exp2"></a>

## func [Exp2](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/exp.go#L140>)

```go
func Exp2(x float64) float64
```

Exp2 returns 2\*\*x, the base\-2 exponential of x.

Special cases are the same as [Exp](<#Exp>).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f\n", math.Exp2(1))
	fmt.Printf("%.2f\n", math.Exp2(-3))
}
```

#### Output

```go
2.00
0.12
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Expm1"></a>

## func [Expm1](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/expm1.go#L126>)

```go
func Expm1(x float64) float64
```

Expm1 returns e\*\*x \- 1, the base\-e exponential of x minus 1. It is more accurate than [Exp](<#Exp>)\(x\) \- 1 when x is near zero.

Special cases are:

```go
Expm1(+Inf) = +Inf
Expm1(-Inf) = -1
Expm1(NaN) = NaN
```

Very large values overflow to \-1 or \+Inf.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.6f\n", math.Expm1(0.01))
	fmt.Printf("%.6f\n", math.Expm1(-1))
}
```

#### Output

```go
0.010050
-0.632121
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="FMA"></a>

## func [FMA](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/fma.go#L95>)

```go
func FMA(x, y, z float64) float64
```

FMA returns x \* y \+ z, computed with only one rounding. \(That is, FMA returns the fused multiply\-add of x, y, and z.\)

<a name="Float32bits"></a>

## func [Float32bits](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/unsafe.go#L24>)

```go
func Float32bits(f float32) uint32
```

Float32bits returns the IEEE 754 binary representation of f, with the sign bit of f and the result in the same bit position. Float32bits\(Float32frombits\(x\)\) == x.

<a name="Float32frombits"></a>

## func [Float32frombits](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/unsafe.go#L30>)

```go
func Float32frombits(b uint32) float32
```

Float32frombits returns the floating\-point number corresponding to the IEEE 754 binary representation b, with the sign bit of b and the result in the same bit position. Float32frombits\(Float32bits\(x\)\) == x.

<a name="Float64bits"></a>

## func [Float64bits](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/unsafe.go#L35>)

```go
func Float64bits(f float64) uint64
```

Float64bits returns the IEEE 754 binary representation of f, with the sign bit of f and the result in the same bit position, and Float64bits\(Float64frombits\(x\)\) == x.

<a name="Float64frombits"></a>

## func [Float64frombits](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/unsafe.go#L41>)

```go
func Float64frombits(b uint64) float64
```

Float64frombits returns the floating\-point number corresponding to the IEEE 754 binary representation b, with the sign bit of b and the result in the same bit position. Float64frombits\(Float64bits\(x\)\) == x.

<a name="Floor"></a>

## func [Floor](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/floor.go#L14>)

```go
func Floor(x float64) float64
```

Floor returns the greatest integer value less than or equal to x.

Special cases are:

```go
Floor(±0) = ±0
Floor(±Inf) = ±Inf
Floor(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	c := math.Floor(1.51)
	fmt.Printf("%.1f", c)
}
```

#### Output

```go
1.0
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Frexp"></a>

## func [Frexp](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/frexp.go#L17>)

```go
func Frexp(f float64) (frac float64, exp int)
```

Frexp breaks f into a normalized fraction and an integral power of two. It returns frac and exp satisfying f == frac × 2\*\*exp, with the absolute value of frac in the interval \[½, 1\).

Special cases are:

```go
Frexp(±0) = ±0, 0
Frexp(±Inf) = ±Inf, 0
Frexp(NaN) = NaN, 0
```

<a name="Gamma"></a>

## func [Gamma](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/gamma.go#L131>)

```go
func Gamma(x float64) float64
```

Gamma returns the Gamma function of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Gamma(+Inf) = +Inf
Gamma(+0) = +Inf
Gamma(-0) = -Inf
Gamma(x) = NaN for integer x < 0
Gamma(-Inf) = NaN
Gamma(NaN) = NaN
```

<a name="Hypot"></a>

## func [Hypot](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/hypot.go#L20>)

```go
func Hypot(p, q float64) float64
```

Hypot returns [Sqrt](<#Sqrt>)\(p\*p \+ q\*q\), taking care to avoid unnecessary overflow and underflow.

Special cases are:

```go
Hypot(±Inf, q) = +Inf
Hypot(p, ±Inf) = +Inf
Hypot(NaN, q) = NaN
Hypot(p, NaN) = NaN
```

<a name="Ilogb"></a>

## func [Ilogb](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/logb.go#L34>)

```go
func Ilogb(x float64) int
```

Ilogb returns the binary exponent of x as an integer.

Special cases are:

```go
Ilogb(±Inf) = MaxInt32
Ilogb(0) = MinInt32
Ilogb(NaN) = MaxInt32
```

<a name="Inf"></a>

## func [Inf](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/bits.go#L20>)

```go
func Inf(sign int) float64
```

Inf returns positive infinity if sign \>= 0, negative infinity if sign \< 0.

<a name="IsInf"></a>

## func [IsInf](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/bits.go#L46>)

```go
func IsInf(f float64, sign int) bool
```

IsInf reports whether f is an infinity, according to sign. If sign \> 0, IsInf reports whether f is positive infinity. If sign \< 0, IsInf reports whether f is negative infinity. If sign == 0, IsInf reports whether f is either infinity.

<a name="IsNaN"></a>

## func [IsNaN](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/bits.go#L34>)

```go
func IsNaN(f float64) (is bool)
```

IsNaN reports whether f is an IEEE 754 “not\-a\-number” value.

<a name="J0"></a>

## func [J0](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/j0.go#L77>)

```go
func J0(x float64) float64
```

J0 returns the order\-zero Bessel function of the first kind.

Special cases are:

```go
J0(±Inf) = 0
J0(0) = 1
J0(NaN) = NaN
```

<a name="J1"></a>

## func [J1](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/j1.go#L75>)

```go
func J1(x float64) float64
```

J1 returns the order\-one Bessel function of the first kind.

Special cases are:

```go
J1(±Inf) = 0
J1(NaN) = NaN
```

<a name="Jn"></a>

## func [Jn](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/jn.go#L54>)

```go
func Jn(n int, x float64) float64
```

Jn returns the order\-n Bessel function of the first kind.

Special cases are:

```go
Jn(n, ±Inf) = 0
Jn(n, NaN) = NaN
```

<a name="Ldexp"></a>

## func [Ldexp](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/ldexp.go#L15>)

```go
func Ldexp(frac float64, exp int) float64
```

Ldexp is the inverse of [Frexp](<#Frexp>). It returns frac × 2\*\*exp.

Special cases are:

```go
Ldexp(±0, exp) = ±0
Ldexp(±Inf, exp) = ±Inf
Ldexp(NaN, exp) = NaN
```

<a name="Lgamma"></a>

## func [Lgamma](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/lgamma.go#L175>)

```go
func Lgamma(x float64) (lgamma float64, sign int)
```

Lgamma returns the natural logarithm and sign \(\-1 or \+1\) of [Gamma](<#Gamma>)\(x\).

Special cases are:

```go
Lgamma(+Inf) = +Inf
Lgamma(0) = +Inf
Lgamma(-integer) = +Inf
Lgamma(-Inf) = -Inf
Lgamma(NaN) = NaN
```

<a name="Log"></a>

## func [Log](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/log.go#L81>)

```go
func Log(x float64) float64
```

Log returns the natural logarithm of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Log(+Inf) = +Inf
Log(0) = -Inf
Log(x < 0) = NaN
Log(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	x := math.Log(1)
	fmt.Printf("%.1f\n", x)

	y := math.Log(2.7183)
	fmt.Printf("%.1f\n", y)
}
```

#### Output

```go
0.0
1.0
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Log10"></a>

## func [Log10](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/log10.go#L9>)

```go
func Log10(x float64) float64
```

Log10 returns the decimal logarithm of x. The special cases are the same as for [Log](<#Log>).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.1f", math.Log10(100))
}
```

#### Output

```go
2.0
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Log1p"></a>

## func [Log1p](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/log1p.go#L96>)

```go
func Log1p(x float64) float64
```

Log1p returns the natural logarithm of 1 plus its argument x. It is more accurate than [Log](<#Log>)\(1 \+ x\) when x is near zero.

Special cases are:

```go
Log1p(+Inf) = +Inf
Log1p(±0) = ±0
Log1p(-1) = -Inf
Log1p(x < -1) = NaN
Log1p(NaN) = NaN
```

<a name="Log2"></a>

## func [Log2](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/log10.go#L22>)

```go
func Log2(x float64) float64
```

Log2 returns the binary logarithm of x. The special cases are the same as for [Log](<#Log>).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.1f", math.Log2(256))
}
```

#### Output

```go
8.0
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Logb"></a>

## func [Logb](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/logb.go#L14>)

```go
func Logb(x float64) float64
```

Logb returns the binary exponent of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Logb(±Inf) = +Inf
Logb(0) = -Inf
Logb(NaN) = NaN
```

<a name="Max"></a>

## func [Max](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/dim.go#L40>)

```go
func Max(x, y float64) float64
```

Max returns the larger of x or y.

Special cases are:

```go
Max(x, +Inf) = Max(+Inf, x) = +Inf
Max(x, NaN) = Max(NaN, x) = NaN
Max(+0, ±0) = Max(±0, +0) = +0
Max(-0, -0) = -0
```

Note that this differs from the built\-in function max when called with NaN and \+Inf.

<a name="Min"></a>

## func [Min](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/dim.go#L76>)

```go
func Min(x, y float64) float64
```

Min returns the smaller of x or y.

Special cases are:

```go
Min(x, -Inf) = Min(-Inf, x) = -Inf
Min(x, NaN) = Min(NaN, x) = NaN
Min(-0, ±0) = Min(±0, -0) = -0
```

Note that this differs from the built\-in function min when called with NaN and \-Inf.

<a name="Mod"></a>

## func [Mod](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/mod.go#L22>)

```go
func Mod(x, y float64) float64
```

Mod returns the floating\-point remainder of x/y. The magnitude of the result is less than y and its sign agrees with that of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Mod(±Inf, y) = NaN
Mod(NaN, y) = NaN
Mod(x, 0) = NaN
Mod(x, ±Inf) = x
Mod(x, NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	c := math.Mod(7, 4)
	fmt.Printf("%.1f", c)
}
```

#### Output

```go
3.0
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Modf"></a>

## func [Modf](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/modf.go#L14>)

```go
func Modf(f float64) (integer float64, fractional float64)
```

Modf returns integer and fractional floating\-point numbers that sum to f. Both values have the same sign as f.

Special cases are:

```go
Modf(±Inf) = ±Inf, NaN
Modf(NaN) = NaN, NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	int, frac := math.Modf(3.14)
	fmt.Printf("%.2f, %.2f\n", int, frac)

	int, frac = math.Modf(-2.71)
	fmt.Printf("%.2f, %.2f\n", int, frac)
}
```

#### Output

```go
3.00, 0.14
-2.00, -0.71
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NaN"></a>

## func [NaN](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/bits.go#L31>)

```go
func NaN() float64
```

NaN returns an IEEE 754 “not\-a\-number” value.

<a name="Nextafter"></a>

## func [Nextafter](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/nextafter.go#L37>)

```go
func Nextafter(x, y float64) (r float64)
```

Nextafter returns the next representable float64 value after x towards y.

Special cases are:

```go
Nextafter(x, x)   = x
Nextafter(NaN, y) = NaN
Nextafter(x, NaN) = NaN
```

<a name="Nextafter32"></a>

## func [Nextafter32](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/nextafter.go#L14>)

```go
func Nextafter32(x, y float32) (r float32)
```

Nextafter32 returns the next representable float32 value after x towards y.

Special cases are:

```go
Nextafter32(x, x)   = x
Nextafter32(NaN, y) = NaN
Nextafter32(x, NaN) = NaN
```

<a name="Pow"></a>

## func [Pow](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/pow.go#L48>)

```go
func Pow(x, y float64) float64
```

Pow returns x\*\*y, the base\-x exponential of y.

Special cases are \(in order\):

```go
Pow(x, ±0) = 1 for any x
Pow(1, y) = 1 for any y
Pow(x, 1) = x for any x
Pow(NaN, y) = NaN
Pow(x, NaN) = NaN
Pow(±0, y) = ±Inf for y an odd integer < 0
Pow(±0, -Inf) = +Inf
Pow(±0, +Inf) = +0
Pow(±0, y) = +Inf for finite y < 0 and not an odd integer
Pow(±0, y) = ±0 for y an odd integer > 0
Pow(±0, y) = +0 for finite y > 0 and not an odd integer
Pow(-1, ±Inf) = 1
Pow(x, +Inf) = +Inf for |x| > 1
Pow(x, -Inf) = +0 for |x| > 1
Pow(x, +Inf) = +0 for |x| < 1
Pow(x, -Inf) = +Inf for |x| < 1
Pow(+Inf, y) = +Inf for y > 0
Pow(+Inf, y) = +0 for y < 0
Pow(-Inf, y) = Pow(-0, -y)
Pow(x, y) = NaN for finite x < 0 and finite non-integer y
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	c := math.Pow(2, 3)
	fmt.Printf("%.1f", c)
}
```

#### Output

```go
8.0
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Pow10"></a>

## func [Pow10](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/pow10.go#L31>)

```go
func Pow10(n int) float64
```

Pow10 returns 10\*\*n, the base\-10 exponential of n.

Special cases are:

```go
Pow10(n) =    0 for n < -323
Pow10(n) = +Inf for n > 308
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	c := math.Pow10(2)
	fmt.Printf("%.1f", c)
}
```

#### Output

```go
100.0
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Remainder"></a>

## func [Remainder](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/remainder.go#L38>)

```go
func Remainder(x, y float64) float64
```

Remainder returns the IEEE 754 floating\-point remainder of x/y.

Special cases are:

```go
Remainder(±Inf, y) = NaN
Remainder(NaN, y) = NaN
Remainder(x, 0) = NaN
Remainder(x, ±Inf) = x
Remainder(x, NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.1f", math.Remainder(100, 30))
}
```

#### Output

```go
10.0
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Round"></a>

## func [Round](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/floor.go#L90>)

```go
func Round(x float64) float64
```

Round returns the nearest integer, rounding half away from zero.

Special cases are:

```go
Round(±0) = ±0
Round(±Inf) = ±Inf
Round(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	p := math.Round(10.5)
	fmt.Printf("%.1f\n", p)

	n := math.Round(-10.5)
	fmt.Printf("%.1f\n", n)
}
```

#### Output

```go
11.0
-11.0
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="RoundToEven"></a>

## func [RoundToEven](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/floor.go#L128>)

```go
func RoundToEven(x float64) float64
```

RoundToEven returns the nearest integer, rounding ties to even.

Special cases are:

```go
RoundToEven(±0) = ±0
RoundToEven(±Inf) = ±Inf
RoundToEven(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	u := math.RoundToEven(11.5)
	fmt.Printf("%.1f\n", u)

	d := math.RoundToEven(12.5)
	fmt.Printf("%.1f\n", d)
}
```

#### Output

```go
12.0
12.0
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Signbit"></a>

## func [Signbit](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/signbit.go#L8>)

```go
func Signbit(x float64) bool
```

Signbit reports whether x is negative or negative zero.

<a name="Sin"></a>

## func [Sin](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/sin.go#L185>)

```go
func Sin(x float64) float64
```

Sin returns the sine of the radian argument x.

Special cases are:

```go
Sin(±0) = ±0
Sin(±Inf) = NaN
Sin(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f", math.Sin(math.Pi))
}
```

#### Output

```go
0.00
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Sincos"></a>

## func [Sincos](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/sincos.go#L16>)

```go
func Sincos(x float64) (sin, cos float64)
```

Sincos returns Sin\(x\), Cos\(x\).

Special cases are:

```go
Sincos(±0) = ±0, 1
Sincos(±Inf) = NaN, NaN
Sincos(NaN) = NaN, NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	sin, cos := math.Sincos(0)
	fmt.Printf("%.2f, %.2f", sin, cos)
}
```

#### Output

```go
0.00, 1.00
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Sinh"></a>

## func [Sinh](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/sinh.go#L26>)

```go
func Sinh(x float64) float64
```

Sinh returns the hyperbolic sine of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Sinh(±0) = ±0
Sinh(±Inf) = ±Inf
Sinh(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f", math.Sinh(0))
}
```

#### Output

```go
0.00
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Sqrt"></a>

## func [Sqrt](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/sqrt.go#L93>)

```go
func Sqrt(x float64) float64
```

Sqrt returns the square root of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Sqrt(+Inf) = +Inf
Sqrt(±0) = ±0
Sqrt(x < 0) = NaN
Sqrt(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	const (
		a = 3
		b = 4
	)
	c := math.Sqrt(a*a + b*b)
	fmt.Printf("%.1f", c)
}
```

#### Output

```go
5.0
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Tan"></a>

## func [Tan](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/tan.go#L83>)

```go
func Tan(x float64) float64
```

Tan returns the tangent of the radian argument x.

Special cases are:

```go
Tan(±0) = ±0
Tan(±Inf) = NaN
Tan(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f", math.Tan(0))
}
```

#### Output

```go
0.00
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Tanh"></a>

## func [Tanh](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/tanh.go#L75>)

```go
func Tanh(x float64) float64
```

Tanh returns the hyperbolic tangent of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Tanh(±0) = ±0
Tanh(±Inf) = ±1
Tanh(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f", math.Tanh(0))
}
```

#### Output

```go
0.00
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Trunc"></a>

## func [Trunc](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/floor.go#L61>)

```go
func Trunc(x float64) float64
```

Trunc returns the integer value of x.

Special cases are:

```go
Trunc(±0) = ±0
Trunc(±Inf) = ±Inf
Trunc(NaN) = NaN
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%.2f\n", math.Trunc(math.Pi))
	fmt.Printf("%.2f\n", math.Trunc(-1.2345))
}
```

#### Output

```go
3.00
-1.00
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Y0"></a>

## func [Y0](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/j0.go#L156>)

```go
func Y0(x float64) float64
```

Y0 returns the order\-zero Bessel function of the second kind.

Special cases are:

```go
Y0(+Inf) = 0
Y0(0) = -Inf
Y0(x < 0) = NaN
Y0(NaN) = NaN
```

<a name="Y1"></a>

## func [Y1](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/j1.go#L156>)

```go
func Y1(x float64) float64
```

Y1 returns the order\-one Bessel function of the second kind.

Special cases are:

```go
Y1(+Inf) = 0
Y1(0) = -Inf
Y1(x < 0) = NaN
Y1(NaN) = NaN
```

<a name="Yn"></a>

## func [Yn](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/jn.go#L235>)

```go
func Yn(n int, x float64) float64
```

Yn returns the order\-n Bessel function of the second kind.

Special cases are:

```go
Yn(n, +Inf) = 0
Yn(n ≥ 0, 0) = -Inf
Yn(n < 0, 0) = +Inf if n is odd, -Inf if n is even
Yn(n, x < 0) = NaN
Yn(n, NaN) = NaN
```

Generated by [gomarkdoc](<https://github.com/princjef/gomarkdoc>)
